title: listia angolensis
intro: Listia angolensis, also known as the African milk tree or milk bush, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and stems are used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Listia angolensis include treating skin conditions, wound healing, and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems are often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
